title: Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution
statement: General relativity predicts that gravitational collapse produces a spacetime singularity where curvature invariants diverge and the classical theory breaks down. The Penrose-Hawking theorems prove such singularities are generic (not artifacts of symmetry), given reasonable energy and causality conditions. What physically replaces the singularity is unknown and is believed to require a quantum theory of gravity, which does not yet exist in complete, predictive form.

originators.name: Karl Schwarzschild
originators.note: 1916 vacuum solution exhibiting the r=0 curvature singularity (and the coordinate singularity at r=2M).

originators.name: Roger Penrose
originators.note: 1965 singularity theorem using trapped surfaces and global methods; Nobel Prize 2020.

originators.name: Stephen Hawking
originators.note: extended the theorems (with Penrose) to cosmology and generic collapse.

ranking.rationale: The deepest and oldest: resolving it requires the missing theory of quantum gravity. Ranked #1 because no other black-hole problem is more fundamental or has resisted longer.

key_ideas: Curvature singularities are where geodesics are incomplete and invariants like the Kretschmann scalar diverge.
Penrose's trapped-surface argument makes singularity formation generic, independent of exact symmetry.
Cosmic censorship (problems #04, #05) concerns whether such singularities are hidden behind horizons.
Candidate resolutions replace r=0 with a quantum-gravity core: a bounce (loop quantum gravity), a fuzzball/string condensate, or a regular (non-singular) black-hole interior.
Any resolution must reduce to classical GR in the appropriate limit and respect the singularity theorems' assumptions or evade one of them (e.g. via quantum energy-condition violation).

approaches.name: Loop quantum gravity / quantum bounce
approaches.summary: Discrete quantum geometry replaces the singularity with a bounce to an expanding region.

approaches.name: String theory / fuzzballs
approaches.summary: The interior is a horizon-scale quantum bound state of strings/branes with no singular core.

approaches.name: Regular (non-singular) black holes
approaches.summary: Effective metrics (Bardeen, Hayward) with a de Sitter core; phenomenological, seeking a fundamental basis.

approaches.name: Asymptotic safety / other QG programs
approaches.summary: Running couplings soften the UV behavior of gravity near the core.

papers.title: Gravitational Collapse and Space-Time Singularities
papers.note: Physical Review Letters 14, 57. The original singularity theorem: trapped surfaces imply geodesic incompleteness. Half of the 2020 Nobel Prize. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: The Singularities of Gravitational Collapse and Cosmology
papers.note: Proceedings of the Royal Society A 314, 529. The general Hawking-Penrose singularity theorem covering both collapse and cosmology. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: Particle Creation by Black Holes
papers.note: Communications in Mathematical Physics 43, 199. Hawking radiation; sets up the information-loss and singularity-endpoint puzzles. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: Black Holes and Entropy
papers.note: Physical Review D 7, 2333. Black-hole entropy proportional to horizon area; foundation of black-hole thermodynamics and the information problem. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: Breakdown of Predictability in Gravitational Collapse
papers.note: Physical Review D 14, 2460. Original statement of the black-hole information-loss paradox. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: Non-singular general-relativistic gravitational collapse (Bardeen regular black hole)
papers.note: Proceedings of GR5 (Tbilisi) conference, 1968. The first regular (singularity-free) black-hole model with a de Sitter-like core; basis of the Bardeen metric. Canonical landmark cited throughout the regular-BH literature.

papers.title: Formation and Evaporation of Regular Black Holes
papers.note: Physical Review Letters 96, 031103. The Hayward regular black hole; a widely used nonsingular model with dynamical formation/evaporation. Canonical landmark.

papers.title: The fuzzball proposal for black holes: an elementary review
papers.note: Fortschritte der Physik 53, 793 (1071 citations). Foundational review of the string-theory fuzzball picture replacing the horizon/singularity with horizonless microstates.

papers.title: Fuzzballs and Microstate Geometries: Black-Hole Structure in String Theory
papers.note: Snowmass white paper, expanded version (51 citations). Current status of the fuzzball/microstate-geometry program.

papers.title: Regular Black Holes: A Short Topic Review
papers.note: International Journal of Theoretical Physics (181 citations). Surveys curvature-regular / geodesically-complete black holes that evade Penrose's theorem by violating the strong energy condition.

papers.title: Some Remarks on Non-Singular Spherically Symmetric Space-Times
papers.note: Astronomy 1, 99. Review of regular black holes with de Sitter cores, black-bounce spacetimes, and the Cauchy-horizon instability issue.

papers.title: On the Inner Horizon Instability of Non-Singular Black Holes
papers.note: Universe 8, 204. Argues the inner (Cauchy) horizon of regular black holes is generically unstable, a key open consistency problem for the regular-BH program.

papers.title: Regular Black Holes with Asymptotically Minkowski Cores
papers.note: Universe 6, 8 (153 citations). Regular-BH models with Minkowski (rather than de Sitter) cores; relates to Bardeen/Hayward/Frolov families.

papers.title: Black hole collapse and bounce in effective loop quantum gravity
papers.note: Classical and Quantum Gravity 37, 235010 (98 citations). LQG effective equations turn Oppenheimer-Snyder collapse into a singularity-free collapse-then-bounce, with BH lifetime ~ (GM)^2/l_Pl.

papers.title: Gravitational collapse in effective loop quantum gravity: Beyond marginally bound configurations
papers.note: Physical Review D 110, 084003. Extends effective-LQG collapse to non-marginally-bound dust; bounce, shock formation, and horizon disappearance.

papers.title: Black-Hole Models in Loop Quantum Gravity
papers.note: Universe 6, 125 (64 citations). Critical review of LQG black-hole interiors; shows many bouncing-interior constructions violate general covariance and discusses signature change.

papers.title: Black hole singularity resolution in Wheeler-DeWitt quantum gravity
papers.note: Annals of Physics (6 citations). Canonical Wheeler-DeWitt quantization of the Schwarzschild interior (Kantowski-Sachs) satisfying the DeWitt criterion for singularity resolution.

papers.title: Black Hole Singularity Resolution in Unimodular Gravity from Unitarity
papers.note: Physical Review Letters (10 citations). Demands unitarity in unimodular time; classical singularity and horizon replaced by a nonsingular quantum region.

papers.title: Black holes in asymptotically safe gravity and beyond
papers.note: Review (42 citations). RG-improved asymptotic-safety black holes: curvature-singularity resolution, inner horizons, cold remnants, and EHT/X-ray constraints on the quantum-gravity scale.

papers.title: Planck stars, White Holes, Remnants and Planck-mass quasi-particles: the quantum gravity phase in black holes' evolution and its manifestations
papers.note: Review (27 citations). Surveys the Planck-star / black-hole-to-white-hole-transition scenario and Planck-mass remnants as dark-matter candidates.

papers.title: Avoidance of Singularity during the Gravitational Collapse with String T-Duality Effects
papers.note: Universe 9, 41 (14 citations). Zero-point-length (string T-duality) effects yield a minimal scale factor and a Planck-star core; correspondence with LQG modified Friedmann equations.

papers.title: The Black Hole Information Problem
papers.note: TASI lectures (174 citations). Pedagogical review of the information paradox, Page curve, AdS/CFT partial resolution, complementarity, and the firewall puzzle.

papers.title: Replica wormholes and the black hole interior
papers.note: Journal of High Energy Physics 2022:205 (235 citations). Derives the Page curve via replica-wormhole geometries in the gravitational path integral; central to the modern information-paradox program.

papers.title: Gravitational Collapse and Cosmic Censorship
papers.note: arXiv:gr-qc/9606016 (194 citations). Review of end-states of gravitational collapse and the open status of the cosmic-censorship hypothesis (black holes vs naked singularities).

papers.title: The influence of Penrose's singularity theorem in general relativity
papers.note: General Relativity and Gravitation 54, 152. Modern retrospective on the 1965 singularity theorem, its developments, common misconceptions, and trapped-surface concepts.

physicists.name: Roger Penrose
physicists.contribution: Proved the first singularity theorem (1965) using trapped surfaces; 2020 Nobel Prize. Originated the cosmic-censorship conjecture.

physicists.name: Stephen W. Hawking
physicists.contribution: Co-proved the Hawking-Penrose singularity theorems; discovered Hawking radiation (1974/75); posed the information-loss paradox (1976).

physicists.name: Jacob D. Bekenstein
physicists.contribution: Introduced black-hole entropy proportional to horizon area, founding black-hole thermodynamics underlying the information problem.

physicists.name: Abhay Ashtekar
physicists.contribution: Co-founder of loop quantum gravity; developed the quantum geometry program used to resolve black-hole and cosmological singularities via a bounce.

physicists.name: Carlo Rovelli
physicists.contribution: Co-founder of loop quantum gravity; proposed Planck stars and the black-hole-to-white-hole transition scenario for singularity/endpoint resolution.

physicists.name: Samir D. Mathur
physicists.contribution: Originated the fuzzball proposal: string-theory microstate geometries replace the horizon and singularity with horizonless structure.

physicists.name: Matt Visser
physicists.contribution: Leading analyst of regular (nonsingular) black holes, black-bounce spacetimes, and inner-horizon (Cauchy) instabilities.

physicists.name: Astrid Eichhorn
physicists.contribution: Leading researcher on asymptotically safe quantum gravity and its RG-improved, curvature-regular black-hole solutions and observational constraints.

physicists.name: Geoffrey Penington
physicists.contribution: Co-developed the quantum-extremal-surface / island and replica-wormhole derivations of the Page curve, reshaping the information-paradox program.

physicists.name: Jose M. M. Senovilla
physicists.contribution: Authority on singularity theorems and trapped surfaces; author of modern reviews clarifying the scope and implications of Penrose's theorem.

simulator_angle: A classical-GR ray tracer cannot probe the singularity, but it can map the trapped region / horizon structure and visualize geodesic incompleteness up to the horizon; regular-black-hole metrics are a drop-in alternative metric to render and compare against Kerr.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 25 verified, genuinely-relevant papers and 10 physicists. Method: ran 12 Consensus paper-search calls (mcp__plugin_bio-research_consensus__search) in batches of three, pacing ~35s between batches to respect rate limits, across varied sub-queries — singularity theorems (Penrose/Hawking), quantum-gravity resolution (Wheeler-DeWitt, unimodular, asymptotic safety, LQG effective collapse, string T-duality), regular/nonsingular black holes (Bardeen/Hayward/de Sitter-core, Minkowski-core, inner-horizon instability), fuzzballs/microstate geometries, information paradox/Page curve/replica wormholes, cosmic censorship, and Planck stars. 15 papers carry live Consensus URLs from search results; the remaining 10 are canonical landmarks I supplied directly with verifiable DOIs/arXiv ids or full bibliographic notes (Penrose 1965, Hawking-Penrose 1970, Hawking 1975 & 1976, Bekenstein 1973, Bardeen 1968, Hayward 2006, Mathur 2005 review, Penington et al. 2022, plus Senovilla 2022). Tiers reflect that this is an OPEN problem: singularity theorems and exact GR are analytic_classical; LQG collapse simulations are numerical_approximation; all resolution-level proposals (regular BHs, fuzzballs, Planck stars, unimodular/Wheeler-DeWitt/asymptotic-safety quantization, islands) are open_conjecture or speculative_extension. No titles, authors, years, DOIs, arXiv ids, or URLs were invented; uncertain items were omitted rather than padded.
